items.headline: Dow Jones Stocks: Honeywell Aerospace Looms As GE Stock Breaks Out
items.summary: Dow Jones stock Honeywell announced the formal board approval of plans to spin out its Honeywell Aerospace unit, as the conglomerate prepares to break up.  Honeywell stock popped above a key level.  On June 29, Honeywell's automation and aerospace businesses will become two independent, publicly traded companies.
items.why_it_matters: News impact is unclear; monitor price and volume reaction.

items.headline: GE Aerospace Rises 12.2% in Six Months: How Should You Play the Stock?
items.summary: GE gains 12.2% in six months as commercial and defense aerospace demand fuel momentum, but costs and valuation remain concerns.
items.why_it_matters: News impact is unclear; monitor price and volume reaction.

items.headline: Wolfspeed Targets High Voltage Growth With Gen 5 SiC And GE Alliance
items.summary: Wolfspeed (NYSE:WOLF) introduced its fifth-generation silicon carbide MOSFET technology, targeting higher efficiency and performance in automotive and industrial power systems. The company also announced an alliance with GE Aerospace to speed up adoption and standardization of high-voltage silicon carbide across advanced industrial and aerospace markets. For readers following power semiconductor trends, Wolfspeed focuses on silicon carbide devices that sit at the heart of electric vehicles,...
items.why_it_matters: News appears more supportive of sentiment or growth expectations.

items.headline: SpaceX has a 'historical analogy' in GE, market historian explains
items.summary: Historian, investor, and author of "How to Get Rich in American History," Joseph Moore, explains the historical precedent for investors to reasonably take a long position in SpaceX (SPCX).

items.headline: Bayer Shares Hinge On Two Court Decisions After $60 Billion Rout
